steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that Knight imitated Hair's fast painting technique, which "accounts in part for the impressionistic qualities synonymous with the group's shared aesthetic." The question asks what logically follows from this.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Knight's paintings embody the impressionistic qualities of the Highwaymen aesthetic': follows directly — adopting Hair's technique means Knight's works share the group's defining impressionistic traits. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Hair's technique is superior to others': introduces a value judgment about quality that the passage does not make. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Reagan's detail-focused works are better': another unsupported quality comparison; the passage describes Reagan's style without ranking it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Knight directly copied Hair's paintings': the passage says Knight imitated Hair's technique, not that she reproduced specific works. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
